message:
fix(alloc,fuse,harness): tighten allocator bounds, simplify FUSE ops, expand conformance tests

- ffs-alloc/lib.rs: Refine bitmap allocator boundary logic for
  improved correctness at edge conditions
- ffs-fuse/lib.rs: Simplify FUSE operation handlers by removing
  redundant intermediate state (-15 lines)
- ffs-harness/conformance.rs: Add 34 new lines of conformance
  test cases for filesystem invariant validation
- Beads issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1t0u","title":"Add edge-case unit tests for repair storage and recovery modules","description":"# Add edge-case unit tests for repair storage and recovery modules

## GOAL
Increase test coverage for the two weakest modules in ffs-repair:
- storage.rs: 5 tests / 974 LOC (1:195 ratio)
- recovery.rs: 6 tests / 915 LOC (1:153 ratio)

## TEST TARGETS

### storage.rs
- Dual-slot descriptor switchover, torn write recovery, generation monotonicity
- Raw vs packed symbol modes, CRC validation round-trips
- Layout validation for edge-case block group sizes

### recovery.rs
- Partial recovery, full corruption, noop recovery
- Evidence record emission, post-repair verification failure

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- storage.rs: ≥12 tests (from 5), recovery.rs: ≥12 tests (from 6)
- All pass, clippy clean","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-18T07:51:00.490788012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T07:58:08.119312106Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T07:58:08.119244249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quality","repair","test"]}
 {"id":"bd-1tz","title":"btrfs walker: reject duplicate node references across traversal","description":"Context: cycle detection landed in bd-382, but duplicate child pointers can still cause repeated traversal/results without forming an ancestor cycle.\n\nProblem:\n- walk_tree currently guards active recursion cycles; it still accepts non-cyclic duplicate logical node references (same child reached twice).\n\nScope:\n- Track globally visited logical nodes across full walk.\n- Return ParseError::InvalidField for duplicate node references.\n- Add regression test for duplicated child pointer case.\n\nAcceptance:\n- Valid trees unchanged.\n- Duplicate node references fail fast with deterministic error.\n- cargo test -p ffs-btrfs passes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyIsland","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:47:59.042964538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:49:06.145413817Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:49:06.145395372Z","close_reason":"Implemented duplicate-node reference detection in btrfs tree walk + regression test; full gates pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","conformance","safety"]}

 {"id":"bd-5s0r.6","title":"Add unit tests for write-back cache DirtyTracker and FlushDaemon","description":"# Add unit tests for write-back cache DirtyTracker and FlushDaemon

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Write-Back Cache epic (bd-5s0r). Currently has E2E test (bd-5s0r.4) but NO unit tests for DirtyTracker invariants or FlushDaemon behavior.

## TEST PLAN

### DirtyTracker Tests
1. Mark block dirty: dirty flag set, dirty count incremented
2. Mark block clean after flush: dirty flag cleared, count decremented
3. Dirty list ordering: oldest dirty block is first
4. Double-mark is idempotent
5. Mark clean on non-dirty block is no-op
6. Dirty ratio calculation is correct

### FlushDaemon Tests
7. Flush batch selects oldest dirty blocks first
8. Flush batch size respects configuration
9. Flush under backpressure: batch size reduced
10. Failed flush retries (block stays dirty)
11. fsync flushes all dirty blocks for specific inode
12. Daemon shutdown completes in-flight flush

### Eviction Coordination Tests
13. Dirty block cannot be evicted without flush
14. Flush failure prevents eviction (block stays in cache)
15. Clean block can be evicted immediately

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 15 tests above, deterministic, mock I/O layer.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log full dirty tracker state

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 15 tests pass
2. Dirty-block-never-evicted invariant verified
3. No flaky tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldenLark","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:50.138555772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:25:33.543548002Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:25:33.543526121Z","close_reason":"unit/integration coverage satisfied; tests green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","test","unit","writeback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:50.138555772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.200119190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.314066429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.6","author":"GoldenLark","text":"Coverage now satisfies this bead via crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs unit tests plus new integration tests. Mapped coverage:\nDirtyTracker semantics: arc_cache_write_through_keeps_dirty_tracker_clean, arc_cache_dirty_blocks_order_oldest_first_and_rewrite_moves_to_tail, cache_metrics_initial_state, cache_metrics_track_hits_and_misses, cache_metrics_list_sizes.\nFlushDaemon semantics: flush_daemon_batch_flushes_oldest_first, flush_daemon_shutdown_flushes_all_dirty_blocks, flush_daemon_flushes_1000_blocks_within_two_intervals, arc_cache_write_back_critical_ratio_triggers_backpressure_flush.\nEviction invariants: arc_cache_explicit_evict_panics_for_dirty_block, arc_cache_explicit_evict_succeeds_for_clean_block, arc_cache_write_back_replacement_succeeds_after_critical_backpressure_flush.\nMVCC+flush lifecycle unit/integration: mvcc_uncommitted_dirty_blocks_are_not_flushed, mvcc_commit_then_flush_marks_persisted_with_pin, mvcc_concurrent_commit_abort_with_daemon_running, plus writeback_e2e scenario suite.\n\nValidation: cargo test -p ffs-block passes with all listed tests green; workspace test/clippy/check/fmt currently green in this snapshot.","created_at":"2026-02-13T20:25:30Z"}]}

- Parser error taxonomy needs explicit layering: ffs-ondisk returns ParseError today; user-facing layers return FfsError.

Audit output should focus on: normative type/trait single-source-of-truth, crate map accuracy vs Cargo.toml, and eliminating phantom contracts/phantom crates.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:33:45Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6st","title":"Harness: Establish parity accounting protocol (FEATURE_PARITY.md <-> ParityReport)","description":"Goal: ensure we never lose track of what is implemented.

Deliverables:
- Define the canonical source of parity totals (FEATURE_PARITY.md) and the canonical source of parity current counts (ffs-harness ParityReport::current).
- Add a lightweight check/test that FEATURE_PARITY.md numbers match the harness report (or the harness reads the file).

Acceptance:
- Any PR that changes implemented functionality updates parity in the same change.
- No more hand-edited divergent numbers.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:18.608385158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:41:07.557633121Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:41:07.557614125Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["harness"]}
 {"id":"bd-6tme","title":"EPIC: Killer Demo — Self-Healing Durability","description":"# EPIC: Killer Demo — Self-Healing Durability

## PURPOSE
Create a compelling, CI-executable demonstration of FrankenFS self-healing: flip random bits in blocks, show automatic repair using RaptorQ symbols, and output a human-readable repair ledger explaining exactly what was reconstructed. This is the "show, don't tell" proof of FrankenFS's core innovation.

## DEMO SCRIPT

### Setup (automated)
1. Create 128MB ext4 image with FrankenFS (RaptorQ enabled, 5% overhead)
2. Write 100 files with known content (photos, text, binaries)
3. Record BLAKE3 checksums for all files
4. Print: "Filesystem created: 100 files, all checksums verified"

### Corruption (visible to audience)
5. Print: "Injecting corruption: flipping random bits in 3% of blocks..."
6. Corrupt 3% of data blocks with random bit flips
7. Print block IDs and bit positions of corrupted blocks
8. Attempt to read files — show which files have corrupted bytes

### Repair (the magic moment)
9. Print: "Triggering self-healing scrub..."
10. Run scrub daemon (or explicit repair command)
11. Show real-time progress: "Scanning block group 1/32... corruption detected at block 1042... repairing with 4 symbols... OK"
12. Print: "Repair complete in 2.3 seconds"

### Verification (proof)
13. Read all 100 files, verify BLAKE3 checksums
14. Print: "All 100 files verified — zero data loss"
15. Print repair ledger: table of every repair action with timing, blocks, symbols used

### Output Format
```
=== FrankenFS Self-Healing Demo ===
Created: 100 files (128MB image, 5% RaptorQ overhead)
Corrupted: 47 blocks across 8 block groups (3% corruption rate)

Repair Results:
  Block Group  Corrupted  Repaired  Symbols Used  Time
  1            3          3         4             12ms
  4            8          8         12            34ms
  7            6          6         8             28ms
  ...
  Total        47         47        72            180ms

Verification: 100/100 files checksums match — ZERO DATA LOSS
Evidence ledger: 47 CorruptionDetected + 47 RepairSucceeded records
```

## REQUIREMENTS
- Must run without root (FUSE user mount)
- Must complete in < 30 seconds
- Must be deterministic (seeded corruption)
- Must be a single command: `cargo run --bin ffs-demo -- self-healing`
- Must work in CI (no GUI, no interactive input)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Demo runs successfully with single command
- [ ] All corrupted blocks repaired
- [ ] Repair ledger is human-readable and correct
- [ ] Demo completes in < 30 seconds
- [ ] Runs in CI without root
- [ ] Output suitable for README/blog post

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. Demo runs with single command in < 30s
3. All corrupted blocks repaired (zero data loss)
4. Output suitable for README/blog post
5. CI-executable without root or FUSE","notes":"DEPENDENCY NOTE: This demo is the capstone of the self-healing story. It depends on bd-15c.11 (5% corruption E2E test) which depends on the full scrub daemon pipeline. Do not attempt this until bd-15c.11 passes. The demo script should reuse the same test infrastructure but with human-readable output formatting. Consider adding a --verbose flag for technical audiences and a --summary flag for non-technical audiences.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"BlueElm","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:44.707195327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T22:11:16.560129014Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T22:11:16.560071136Z","close_reason":"All children completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","selfhealing","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tme","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:31.502093882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":97,"issue_id":"bd-6tme","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:41Z"},{"id":167,"issue_id":"bd-6tme","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: repair_durability
Mapped graveyard sections: 1.1 RaptorQ + 12.13 Change-point + 11.11 PoR
EV score: 25 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: decode_budget_ms=200, io_budget_mb=64, max_repair_attempts=3
Fallback trigger: read-only degrade + evidence ledger + manual repair workflow

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6tme.1","title":"Adoption wedge: single-command corruption recovery demo for README","description":"# Adoption wedge: single-command corruption recovery demo for README

## GOAL
Create the simplest possible adoption wedge: a single command that demonstrates FrankenFS self-healing in under 10 seconds, suitable for copy-paste into a README or blog post.

## BACKGROUND
From alien_cs_graveyard.md methodology: every graveyard entry must have an adoption wedge -- the smallest artifact that proves the idea works and makes skeptics try it. bd-6tme (Killer Demo) is the full 30-second version. This bead is the MINIMAL wedge: the thing someone runs in 5 seconds to see the value proposition.

## DELIVERABLES
1. `cargo run --example self_heal_demo` -- creates 8MB image, writes 10 files, corrupts 2%, repairs, verifies
2. Output: 6 lines of text showing create -> corrupt -> repair -> verify cycle
3. Zero dependencies beyond the workspace (no FUSE mount needed -- operates on raw image)
4. Runs in CI, deterministic, < 5 seconds

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: demo script exits 0 and output matches expected pattern
2. Integration: verify checksums correct after repair

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Demo start (info): image_size, file_count, corruption_pct
- Corruption injected (info): blocks_corrupted, seed
- Repair complete (info): blocks_repaired, duration_ms
- Verification (info): files_verified, all_ok

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Single command, zero config, < 5 seconds
2. Output is copy-paste-ready for README
3. Deterministic (seeded)
4. CI-executable (no root, no FUSE)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SandyEagle","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:00.301279553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T03:27:58.887605459Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T03:27:58.887586904Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","selfhealing","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tme.1","depends_on_id":"bd-6tme","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:00.301279553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":123,"issue_id":"bd-6tme.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
